title: Shinjis Back SECURITY
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Back?! SECURITY...!!!             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:01 pm There's one part I find quite 'unrealistic' or 'doesn't make sense': Chapter 19"We have an EMARGENCY" Scene: After cutting all ties with NERV & returning the 'High Security' pass card etc.; suddenly 'barged in' during Angel Zeruel's attack & demands to pilot EVA 01..... Question: Didn't he cut all ties with NERV & returned the his 'High Security' pass card & all..., how did he get through security... or the front door for that matter...??!! Security on holiday that day...? Ran away cos Zeruel is so frightening...? Shinji found a 'back door'...?

[]: Re: Shinji's Back?! SECURITY...!!!             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:23 pm pen-pen wrote: There's one part I find quite 'unrealistic' or 'doesn't make sense': Chapter 19"We have an EMARGENCY" Scene: After cutting all ties with NERV & returning the 'High Security' pass card etc.; suddenly 'barged in' during Angel Zeruel's attack & demands to pilot EVA 01..... Question: Didn't he cut all ties with NERV & returned the his 'High Security' pass card & all..., how did he get through security... or the front door for that matter...??!! Security on holiday that day...? Ran away cos Zeruel is so frightening...? Shinji found a 'back door'...?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:24 pm I think the Nerv personal that were still manning their posts said "to hell with protocol, we're getting our asses handed to us by this Angel so let the damn kid through or die for sure in a few minutes." Indeed, if anything makes no sense it's that there are no Nerv personnel there to open the doors and let the kids through then guide them to the Eva's in episode 11. (And episode 1 for that matter, though Misato probably didn't let on that she didn't understand the map still. It was an emargency, though... ...). Anyway, about the guards, we do see them in EoE and IIRC at least one of the episodes. It only makes sense that they would have manned personnel, at least one or two, at every major entrance like the one the kids were stuck at in 11. I do love that episode, but plothole methinks.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:28 pm The Zeruel battle left Nerv and the Geofront in a chaotic shambles. It's totally feasible that Shinji could have gotten inside; and if I'm not mistaken, the Geofront has civilian shelters inside it (or maybe that's just Rebuild screwing up my memory). As for where security was, that's obvious: They were in the LCL plant looting Rei clones.

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:48 pm SAWgunner wrote: The Zeruel battle left Nerv and the Geofront in a chaotic shambles. It's totally feasible that Shinji could have gotten inside; and if I'm not mistaken, the Geofront has civilian shelters inside it (or maybe that's just Rebuild screwing up my memory). As for where security was, that's obvious: They were in the LCL plant looting Rei clones.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:55 pm SAWgunner wrote: They were in the LCL plant looting Rei clones.

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:21 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Relevant: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 04, 2011 9:51 pm Nice! I'd ask for a translation but I don't think it needs one.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Jul 05, 2011 3:29 pm The shelter he was in was next to the Geo-Front and the wall got busted in because Unit 02's fucking head came flying through.

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Tue Jul 05, 2011 9:52 pm Trajan wrote: The shelter he was in was next to the Geo-Front and the wall got busted in because Unit 02's fucking head came flying through.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jul 05, 2011 11:23 pm He just killed the one guy standing guard at the gate. Which EOE tells us somehow makes all the doors open...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jul 05, 2011 11:29 pm pen-pen wrote: Good theory......

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jul 05, 2011 11:35 pm You'd think there would be multiple levels of security clearance, though. Just anybody who's in the garden can waltz right up next to Unit-01? Seems lax. Besides, we learn when Shinji delivers the new card to Rei that there's at least one more check point.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Jul 05, 2011 11:51 pm Warren Peace wrote: You'd think there would be multiple levels of security clearance, though. Just anybody who's in the garden can waltz right up next to Unit-01? Seems lax. Besides, we learn when Shinji delivers the new card to Rei that there's at least one more check point.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jul 05, 2011 11:53 pm Sure, but I don't see how that would make any difference as to whether or not he could get all the way to Unit-01...

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Wed Jul 06, 2011 12:34 am Legendary wrote: It's not a theory. It is exactly what happens in the show. Shinji sees Eva-02's head, freaks out, and heads through the hole in the wall into the Geofront proper where Kaji is. There's no security because the hole isn't Nerv-approved and anyone inside the gardens SHOULD have clearance to access most Nerv facilities...

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Wed Jul 06, 2011 2:27 am The other thing that doesn't make sense is: Why is a civilian shelter or a watermelon patch situated so close to NERV(a high security top secret area)?? If its not, then it would've taken Shinji a while to 'travel'(by taxi or train) to NERV, go through the security-less front door, into the huge maze-like-till-recently-Misato-still gets-lost NERV center again without security, then to the hangar where they keep the highly out-of-bounds Biotech Robot Killing Machine EVA- 01...you've guessed it- without security....

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jul 06, 2011 2:30 am To answer just about every question asked in this thread: because the plot demands it. Does it make sense how Shinji made it in? No. Does it need to make sense? Not really.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 06, 2011 10:28 am The shelters are there because it's relatively safe down there/the most/best defended place in the city. The melon patch is there because, uh, well, apparently, the Geofront can support plant life somehow, and wouldn't it be practical to have your melons/the place where you spend your free time next to where you work when your job consists of saving the world? As for Shinji getting in, the NERV ppl display quite the degree of crazy preparedness thorought the series... I'd say that he found one of these bellbuttons with a speaker next to them and he told the guy on the other end who he is. "Please, please, you have to let me in, or else, everybody I know/Ayanami (depending on continuity) will die!"

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jul 06, 2011 12:39 pm He used the hilarity of the inadvertantly murdered baby to slip past security.
